project_org_chart.select_project_placeholder: Select a project to view the org chart

project_workspace_prototype.page_description: Prototype workspace for testing project governance UX, role structures, evidence flow, and audit navigation.

software_delivery.label: Software Delivery
software_delivery.description: Use this template to evaluate roles such as PM, SWA, SWT, CM, QA, DEV, and release-related checkpoints.

compliance_audit.label: Compliance Audit
compliance_audit.description: Use this template to prototype audit preparation, controlled evidence, approval flow, and corrective action structure.

process_improvement.label: Process Improvement
process_improvement.description: Use this template to test process rollout, pilot teams, improvement roles, and governance transitions.

compact_team.label: Compact Team
compact_team.description: A small delivery team focused on core execution roles with lightweight governance.

standard_team.label: Standard Team
standard_team.description: A balanced project team with architecture, quality, business analysis, and delivery coverage.

release_readiness.label: Release Readiness
release_readiness.description: A governance-heavy scenario focused on release gates, approvals, configuration control, and evidence readiness.

audit_preparation.label: Audit Preparation
audit_preparation.description: A scenario centered on audit preparation, evidence consolidation, sign-off readiness, and controlled records.

corrective_action.label: Corrective Action
corrective_action.description: A scenario focused on remediation ownership, closure tracking, and controlled process updates.

process_rollout.label: Process Rollout
process_rollout.description: A scenario for organization-wide rollout planning, readiness review, and release of process assets.

pilot_team.label: Pilot Team
pilot_team.description: A smaller improvement team for piloting process changes before broader rollout.

project_type.title: Project Type
project_type.helper: Classification currently applied to this project

active_members.title: Active Members
active_members.helper: People currently assigned to the project

active_roles.title: Project Roles
active_roles.helper: Roles currently defined for this project

reporting_roots.title: Reporting Roots
reporting_roots.helper: Top-level members in the project org chart

overview.workspace_prompts_title: Questions to Validate in This Scenario
overview.callout_description: This workspace is intentionally driven by template and scenario presets so the team can validate navigation, terminology, and governance flow before committing to live section behavior.

org_chart.title: Project Reporting Structure
org_chart.description: Switch between hierarchy view and role-grouped view to validate which perspective is easier to use.

workflow.title: Workflow Preview
workflow.description: Use this section to validate how project roles, approval gates, and required evidence should flow before wiring live workflow behavior.
workflow.state_flow_description: Use this view to validate whether the item should move through the right governance states for the selected project size.

default.1: The next gate should stop when a required review or evidence pack is still incomplete.

large_project.1: Large projects should not proceed to release until the quality gate and board review are both complete.
large_project.2: A missing governance register should block the final release gate for large projects.

release_readiness.1: Release cannot proceed while reviewer or approver coverage is still incomplete.
release_readiness.2: The release package should remain blocked until the milestone snapshot is available.

audit_preparation.1: Audit preparation should stop when the audit pack is not complete or controlled records are missing.
audit_preparation.2: The approval chain should not close until evidence ownership is explicit for every stage.

questions.1: Is it obvious which role is expected to review, approve, or release at each step?
questions.2: Can the team understand which evidence must exist before the next gate opens?
questions.3: Would a project lead know where to click next when a gate is blocked?

owner.title: Project owner is assigned
owner.detail: The project already has a clearly identified owner.

role_gap.title: Role coverage still has gaps
role_gap.detail: At least one role in this scenario is intentionally marked as a gap so the team can test how compliance issues should guide users.

org_chart.title: Project org chart is defined
org_chart.detail: The reporting structure already shows a clear top-level owner and reporting lines.

evidence.title: Evidence package is not complete
evidence.detail: The release evidence and milestone snapshot are still pending and should be prepared before audit review.

governance_register.title: Governance register is still incomplete
governance_register.detail: Large projects should consolidate role ownership, sign-offs, and readiness notes in a governance register.

audit_pack.title: Audit preparation pack is still incomplete
audit_pack.detail: This scenario should surface an audit-ready bundle before the audit trail is handed over.

large_project_snapshot.title: Large project governance snapshot is still incomplete
large_project_snapshot.detail: A large project should freeze a governance snapshot before final release review.

audit_preparation_snapshot.title: Audit snapshot has not been frozen
audit_preparation_snapshot.detail: Audit preparation should include a dated snapshot of evidence ownership and approval state.

release_readiness_gate.title: Release gate still depends on final snapshot approval
release_readiness_gate.detail: The release readiness review should not close until the release candidate snapshot is accepted.

evidence.title: Evidence Packages
evidence.snapshot_preview_description: Use snapshots to validate what the auditor or project lead should see at a fixed point in time.

snapshots.title: Snapshot Preview
snapshots.m1_detail: Early planning baseline, approved role ownership, and initial evidence map.
snapshots.m2_detail: Verification checkpoint with updated approvals, open actions, and evidence gaps.
snapshots.release_detail: Final readiness cut used before release board or approval gate.
snapshots.audit_detail: Controlled snapshot prepared specifically for auditor walkthrough and evidence review.

synthetic.audit_pack_description: Controlled evidence package for internal and external audit preparation.
synthetic.governance_register_description: Cross-role register that consolidates stage owners, sign-offs, and readiness notes.

modal.usage_text: Use this preview to validate what an evidence package should show before wiring live export and snapshot behavior.

auditor_mode.title: Auditor Mode
auditor_mode.description: This view prioritizes compliance, evidence, workflow controls, and audit trail so audit discussions can move faster.

side_panels.prototype_notes_1: The selected project only provides business context at the top of the workspace.
side_panels.prototype_notes_2: The main sections are driven by template and scenario presets so role patterns can be evaluated quickly.
side_panels.prototype_notes_3: Use these presets to validate terms such as SWT, SWA, CM, QA, and approval roles before wiring live project behavior.

default.1: Can users immediately understand who owns this project and which roles are still missing?
default.2: Does the workspace make it obvious where to click next when a role or evidence gap exists?
default.3: Would an auditor or QA reviewer understand the project structure without extra explanation?

release_readiness.1: Can the team quickly see whether release roles such as CM, RVW, and APR are covered?
release_readiness.2: Is it clear which evidence pack should be reviewed before the release gate?
release_readiness.3: Does the org chart make approval and reporting lines obvious before release?

audit_preparation.1: Is the evidence pack easy to inspect before an ISO or CMMI review?
audit_preparation.2: Can the team identify who is responsible for sign-off and who is preparing controlled records?
audit_preparation.3: Does the compliance section show gaps clearly enough for audit preparation?

corrective_action.1: Can users see who owns each remediation responsibility and who verifies closure?
corrective_action.2: Is the path from evidence to audit trail clear enough for corrective-action follow-up?
corrective_action.3: Does the role model reflect how process changes move from action to controlled release?

project_evidence.page_description: Review team structure, role responsibilities, and assignment history as auditable project evidence.

owner_assigned.description: The project should have a clearly assigned owner.

sponsor_assigned.title: Project sponsor assigned

detail.warning: Assign a sponsor for this project type.

primary_assignment_exists.title: Primary assignment exists

document_creator_role.description: A role with document creation rights should be actively assigned.

detail.failed: Assign a member to a role that can create documents.

review_role_assigned.title: Review role assigned
review_role_assigned.description: A role with review capability should be actively assigned.

detail.warning: Assign a member to a review-capable role.

approval_role_assigned.title: Approval role assigned
approval_role_assigned.description: A role with approval capability should be actively assigned.

detail.warning: Assign a member to an approval-capable role.

release_role_assigned.title: Release role assigned
release_role_assigned.description: A role with release capability should be actively assigned when controlled release is needed.

detail.warning: Consider assigning a release-capable role if controlled release applies.

documents.welcome: Document operations are reserved for assigned document roles only.
documents.operations_description: Only users with document-specific roles should upload files, manage versions, publish documents, delete drafts, or deactivate active documents.
documents.read_only_description: You can review document records, but lifecycle actions are restricted to document roles.

upload.title: Upload Draft
upload.description: Upload a new draft file through the backend storage flow.

version.title: Change Version
version.description: Create or replace a document version when versioning flow is enabled.

publish.title: Publish
publish.description: Declare a reviewed document as active for controlled use.

delete_draft.title: Delete Draft
delete_draft.description: Remove draft documents before they are released.

deactivate.title: Deactivate
deactivate.description: Cancel active use when a document is obsolete or superseded.

upload.allowed_file_types: Allowed file types: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX
upload.invalid_type_description: Only PDF, Word, and Excel documents can be uploaded.
upload.file_required_description: Please choose a document file before saving.

upload_page.title: Upload New Document
upload_page.description: Provide a document name and select the file to upload.

form.note: This is a local test form in documents module (no API call yet).

activity_logs.title: Activity Logs
activity_logs.description: Review user and system activity by module, actor, affected record, and request outcome.

audit_logs.title: Audit Logs
audit_logs.description: Review audit evidence with change snapshots, request context, and compliance-grade trace details.
